assertions.text: Output contains at least one markdown table
assertions.evidence:
## Table 1: Order Status Transition

| Scenario | Current Status | Target Status | Allowed? | Reason? |
|---|---|---|---|---|

assertions.text: Table covers cancellation rules — includes rows for states where cancellation is allowed and where it is not
assertions.evidence: | Cancel before shipment | {PENDING, CONFIRMED} | CANCELLED | yes | | | Cancel after dispatch | SHIPPED | CANCELLED | no | Already dispatched | | Cancel delivered order | DELIVERED | CANCELLED | no | Use return process instead |

assertions.text: The 30-day return window rule is addressed — either as a threshold column, a separate table, or flagged as an open question
assertions.evidence:
## Table 2: Return Eligibility

| Scenario | Order Status | Days Since Delivery | Return Window (Policy) | Eligible? | Reason? |

assertions.text: Uses value sets {PENDING, CONFIRMED} or equivalent to express 'regardless of which starting state' for cases where a rule holds across states
assertions.evidence: | Cancel before shipment | {PENDING, CONFIRMED} | CANCELLED | yes | |

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: | Scenario | Current Status | Target Status | Allowed? | Reason? |
